exemplar.title: Modelling a beauty blender and finding its volume

detailedExemplarData.title: Modelling a beauty blender and finding its volume

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear overall structure with introduction, body and conclusion
Consistent use of headings, subheadings, figures and appendices
weaknesses: Some redundancy in full polynomial expansions and repeated formulae
Occasional jumps in logic and digressions into procedural details
Minor coherence lapses such as a cut-off sentence in the procedure
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and logically developed, with well-organized structure and mostly relevant content. Minor lapses in conciseness (redundant algebra, unnecessary screenshots) and occasional small logic jumps prevent it from reaching the top band, so a band 3 award is justified.

strengths: Correct use of Ï, integral and derivative notation in many places
Inclusion of multiple representations (graphs, tables, photographs)
Reasonable logical progression in many explanations
weaknesses: Inconsistent spacing, power notation and unit usage (ml vs cmÂ³)
Unlabelled or cropped axes on figures reduce interpretability
Long algebraic blocks lack explanatory commentary; unexplained switches between Ï and 2Ï
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are used appropriately at times but inconsistently, and although there are multiple representations, their integration and labelling are only partially effective. The logical clarity is present but not sustained throughout, aligning with band 2.

strengths: Choice of a personal makeup tool and experimental water-displacement test show clear initiative
Personal interest drives the exploration and informs modelling choices
Good creativity in combining theoretical calculus with empirical validation
weaknesses: Mathematical approach follows standard classroom techniques rather than novel methods
Single experimental trial limits reliability of findings
Could have extended experimentation (e.g., multiple trials) for deeper engagement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is significant evidence of independent thinking, a clear personal approach and a good level of creativity, but the work does not demonstrate the outstanding or exceptional engagement required for the top band, so a band 2 is appropriate.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on discrepancies between theoretical and experimental volumes
Thoughtful identification of error sources (scale precision, curve-fitting)
Clear evaluation with suggestions for improving the model and experiment
weaknesses: Reflection points are relatively brief and not fully integrated throughout
Lacks quantification of measurement uncertainty and error propagation
Could include deeper critical analysis (e.g., percentage error effects, boundary continuity)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection and thoughtful analysis with a clear evaluation of outcomes, but the depth and critical insight fall short of substantial reflection, placing it in band 2.

strengths: Appropriate use of SL calculus techniques: polynomial fitting, definite integration, differentiation
Integration of GeoGebra fitting and experimental comparison demonstrates sound application
Mathematics is relevant and generally applied correctly to the aim of finding volume and surface area
weaknesses: Algebraic expansions and derivative calculations contain transcription errors
Unjustified switches between Ï and 2Ï and rounding inconsistencies
Final theoretical volume differs substantially from experimental result due to calculation flaws
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics used is relevant, commensurate with the SL level and shows good understanding, but errors in algebraic steps and inconsistencies in notation prevent it from reaching the effective and error-free application of band 4, so a mid-band 3 score is warranted.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments usually proceed in a logical order, but long algebraic blocks without explanatory commentary and the unexplained switch between Ï and 2Ï reduce clarity.  Consequently, the communication demonstrates âsome logical structure and clarityâ rather than the consistent clarity demanded for band 3.

The student demonstrates understanding of integration and differentiation formulas, but algebraic expansions contain transcription errors, the switch between Ï and 2Ï is unjustified, and the final theoretical volume differs greatly from the experimental measure.  These flaws indicate âsome understanding with errorsâ, meriting band 2.

Graphs of the outline, polynomial equations, numerical tables (integration results) and photographs constitute more than one representation, but they are not always integrated or clearly labelled (e.g. axes on figures are cropped).  Therefore usage is present yet only partially effective, aligning with band 2.

The student structures the report in a clear sequence (Introduction â Modelling â Volume â Surface Area â Experimental test â Evaluation).  Explanations normally follow from one another, so the reader can follow the line of thought without major difficulty.  Minor lapsesâsuch as digressions into repeated algebraic expansions and occasional jumps in logicâprevent the piece from reaching the âhighly coherentâ descriptor, but overall the logical development is solid and sustained.

Using GeoGebra fitting, designing an experimental validation, and combining theoretical with empirical volumes show a good degree of creativity and initiative, but not to an âexceptionalâ level.  Band 2 therefore matches the evidence.

The student analyses the discrepancy between calculated (18 cmÂ³) and experimental (38 cmÂ³) volumes, offering plausible sources of error such as scale precision and curve-fitting issues.  The analysis is thoughtful yet not deeply probing, fitting the âthoughtful analysisâ band.

An explicit evaluation of outcomes is provided, with suggestions for adding more points, using electronic scales and analysing other blender shapes.  The discussion considers implications but remains concise, matching the descriptor âclear evaluation of outcomes and implicationsâ (band 2).

Reflection appears in both the Experimental section and the Evaluation, touching on measurement uncertainty, modelling limitations and potential improvements.  This constitutes âmeaningful reflectionâ, but the comments are relatively brief and not woven throughout, so the work does not reach âsubstantial critical reflectionâ.

Correct symbols for Ï, integral signs and derivatives are used, but spacing, powers and multiplication dots are inconsistent and sometimes replaced by plain text ("0.61x^3").  Units are occasionally confused (ml â¡ cmÂ³).  Hence there is âsome appropriate mathematical language and notationâ rather than consistent accuracy.

The studentâs interest in cosmetics drives the entire exploration, and personal decisions (number of points, accuracy trade-offs, experimental check) reveal a clear personal approach.  The work does not introduce novel mathematical techniques, so the level is best described by band 2.

Most content is relevant to finding volume and surface area, but there is noticeable redundancy: full polynomial expansions, repeated writing of identical formulae and unnecessary screenshots add bulk without new insight.  These extraneous elements hinder conciseness, so the work fits the descriptor âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ.

Polynomial fitting, definite integration for solids and surface area, and an experimental check are all directly relevant to the aim, showing appropriate deployment of SL calculus.  While effective in places, occasional misuse of 2Ï and rounding issues stop it from being âeffectiveâ enough for band 4; therefore band 3 is appropriate.

Choosing a personal makeup tool, physically cutting and measuring it, and performing an independent water-displacement test all show notable initiative beyond classroom examples, indicating âsignificant evidence of independent thinkingâ.  However, the mathematics itself closely follows standard techniques, so the evidence is not outstanding.

Headings, sub-headings, figures and appendices are used consistently, giving the exploration a recognisable introduction, body and conclusion.  The material inside some headings (e.g. the lengthy algebra in Appendices) is not always placed in the best location, yet the overall skeleton is unmistakably appropriate and easy to navigate, meriting the âwell-organised with clear structureâ band.

Integration of non-trivial polynomials, differentiation for surface area, and use of GeoGebra fitting all lie comfortably at or slightly above the SL syllabus, and are applied in context.  Thus the mathematics is âfully commensurate with course levelâ, corresponding to band 4.

annotations.comment: The introduction effectively links personal experience with a clear mathematical aim, demonstrating genuine curiosity about the design and real-world application of calculus.
The narrative in the modelling description is overly verbose and repeats details about image editing; consider condensing to focus on the mathematical process and measurements.
The last sentence is cut off mid-description; ensure the full procedure (e.g., placing the image on GeoGebra) is clearly stated to maintain coherence.
Axes in Figure 1 are cropped and unlabeled; include axis labels and scales to make the graph fully interpretable.
Defining 10 points is a good start, but the naming convention (CâL) should be introduced with a small table or key for reader orientation.
The rationale for rounding to two decimal places is given, but quantifying its effect on final volume (e.g., percentage error) would strengthen the justification.
Code notation in the FitPoly block is inconsistent (e.g., â\times^3â and stray commas); use proper LaTeX formatting and standard function notation for clarity.
The verbal explanation of restricting each polynomial section is clear, but consider adding a brief note on continuity at the boundaries of each piecewise segment.
Intermediate algebraic steps for (0.61xÂ³â1.53xÂ²+1.87x)Â² contain repeated terms and missing simplifications; re-derive carefully to avoid arithmetic errors.
The switch between using Ï and 2Ï in volume calculations is not explained; maintain consistent notation and comment on its use in the shell vs disc method if relevant.
Summarizing all polynomial integrations in the main body (instead of simply referring to the appendix) would improve logical flow and help the reader follow the cumulative volume calculation.
The derivative step labels coefficients incorrectly (using â3Ã0.31â instead of â3Ã0.61â); recalculate and verify derivative coefficients to ensure accuracy.
Surface area units (cmÂ²) should be explicitly stated in the integral setup to avoid ambiguity, rather than appended only to the final answer.
Confusion arises from using âmlâ and âcmÂ³â interchangeably in the experimental section; standardize units and explain equivalence for clarity.
Integrating an experimental water-displacement test shows reflective awareness of model limitations and strengthens the evaluation of theoretical results.
The conclusion briefly states use of the mean experimental and theoretical volume; consider discussing why averaging is appropriate and its limitations.
Consider integrating measurement uncertainty from the ruler and scale into the model parameters themselves to link experimental error with theoretical predictions.
Replicating the water displacement measurement and reporting multiple trials would demonstrate stronger engagement and improve reliability of the experimental check.
Discuss the measurement uncertainty in the water displacement method (e.g., Â±1 ml scale error) and propagate this into a volume range to deepen reflection.
The growth calculation uses â(final width â final length)â instead of â(final width â initial width)â; revise to correctly compute dimensional changes.
Limitations are acknowledged well, but would benefit from quantifying the effect of cutting error on the volume (e.g., Â±0.05 cm in dimensions giving Â±x cmÂ³ in volume).
